summary: Completed testing of MapChap Telegram Mini App - Backend APIs working perfectly (100% success), Frontend deployment has asset path configuration issue preventing proper loading

ui_bugs.issue: Vue.js app not loading due to incorrect asset paths in built HTML
ui_bugs.selector: Assets referenced as '/assets/' but located at '/mapchap-frontend/assets/'
ui_bugs.details: Built index.html references '/assets/index-C7T9xyeD.js' and '/assets/index-C_ayxXl-.css' but files are at '/mapchap-frontend/assets/'. This is a Vite build configuration issue - base path needs to be set correctly.

integration_issues.issue: Vue app div exists but remains hidden due to failed asset loading
integration_issues.details: HTML loads successfully but CSS and JS assets fail to load with 404 errors, preventing Vue app from mounting

passed_tests: Create Offer API - POST /api/offers (coordinates saved as [lat, lng])
Get All Offers API - GET /api/offers (4 offers with proper coordinates)
Asset Files Exist (CSS and JS files present in bucket)

action_item_for_main_agent.critical_issue: Frontend deployment configuration needs immediate fix
action_item_for_main_agent.root_cause: Vite build configuration missing correct base path for Yandex Object Storage deployment
action_item_for_main_agent.required_fix: Update vite.config.js to set base: '/mapchap-frontend/' or configure bucket to serve from root with proper index document
action_item_for_main_agent.backend_status: ✅ All APIs working perfectly - business flow, coordinates, verification all functional
action_item_for_main_agent.frontend_status: ❌ CRITICAL - App not loading due to asset path mismatch
specific_actions: Fix Vite build configuration to use correct base path
Redeploy frontend with proper asset paths
Alternatively, configure Yandex Object Storage bucket to serve index.html as default document
